title: cyathea incana
intro: Cyathea incana is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive fronds and ability to thrive in shaded, moist environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Cyathea incana include relief from digestive issues, pain management, and immune system support. Cyathea incana is often prepared as a tea or decoction by boiling the roots or fronds in water, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
